title: Do not use waitlist CTAs without product context
summary: AI-generated marketing pages use 'Join the waitlist' as a standalone hero CTA without explaining what the product does or why waiting is worthwhile.
instruction: When using a waitlist or early-access CTA, pair it with specific product context: what the product does, what waitlist access means, and a reason to join now rather than later.
rationale: A bare 'Join the Waitlist' CTA provides no information for the user's decision. Context-specific waitlist copy ('Join 5,000+ designers waiting for our Figma plugin') gives users a reason to act and sets expectations.

violations: A landing page hero with 'Join the Waitlist' as the only CTA and no product description or value proposition.
A splash page with only an email input and 'Get Early Access' button, no context about the product.
controls: A waitlist page explaining 'We are building a collaborative design tool for product teams. Join 5,000+ designers on the waitlist for early access in Q3.'
A product tease with timeline and benefit: 'Private beta starts March 2026. Early access includes priority support and discounted lifetime pricing.'
borderline: A waitlist for a well-known company's sequel product where brand recognition provides sufficient context.

exceptions.description: Teaser campaigns for known brands where the product is already understood from context. Confirmed-viral referrals where the network is the value.
examples: Invite-only social platform with known reputation

detector.signalDescription: Find waitlist CTA patterns ('Join the waitlist', 'Get early access', 'Sign up for beta'). Check for nearby product-description context within the same section.
limitations: Context may be visually present but not structurally adjacent in DOM.
Known brands may not need product description because context is assumed.

scenarios: A known brand launching a clearly understood product extension.
An internal tool with a known audience that already understands the product.
mitigations: Check for brand-name recognition in page title or navigation as implicit context.

copyPattern.replacementStrategy: Pair waitlist CTAs with specific context: what the product does, timeline, benefits of early access, or the community already waiting.

fixturePlan.description: A landing page hero with 'Join the Waitlist' button and no product description.
Same page with product description, timeline, and community count before the waitlist CTA.
A known company's product teaser where brand provides sufficient context.
